- During the operation of the system, in case of any severe failures that seriously affect system stability, the monitoring and alarm functions we provide will play a crucial role. These functions are designed to offer vendors a convenient system information monitoring service, enabling them to obtain real-time system information at any time through compatible terminals.

- If you want to know the detailed content of monitoring signals, such as the specific other types supported, how to customize monitoring signals, or if you have any questions during the use process, please contact our professional support team.

# Inverter Alarm Code
| Alarm code | Description | Severity Level |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1001 | Software version mismatch. | Critical |
| 1002 | Low insulation resistance. | Critical |
| 1003 | The temperature is too high. | Critical |
| 1004 | Equipment failure. | Critical |
| 1005 | The system grounding is abnormal. | Critical |
| 1006 | PV string voltage is high. | Critical |
| 1007 | PV string reverse connection. | Critical |
| 1008 | PV string back-filling. | Critical |
| 1009 | AFCI fault. | Critical |
| 1010 | Grid outage. | Critical |
| 1011 | Grid overvoltage. | Critical |
| 1012 | Grid undervoltage. | Critical |
| 1013 | Grid overfrequency. | Critical |
| 1014 | Grid underfrequency. | Critical |
| 1015 | Grid voltage imbalance 1. | Critical |
| 1016 | The DC component of the output current exceeds the limits. | Critical |
| 1017 | The leakage electricity exceeds the Limits. | Critical |
| 1018 | Communication abnormal. | General |
| 1019 | System internal protection. | Critical |
| 1020 | AFCI self-test circuit fault. | Critical |
| 1021 | Off-grid protection. | Critical |

# Battery Alarm Code
| Alarm code | Description | Severity Level |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 2001 | Software version mismatch. | Critical |
| 2002 | The energy storage module has low insulation resistance to ground. | General |
| 2003 | The temperature is too high. | Critical |
| 2004 | Equipment failure. | Critical |
| 2005 | Below desired temperature. | Critical |
| 2008 | System internal protection. | Critical |

# Gateway Alarm Code
| Alarm code | Description | Severity Level |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 3001 | Software version mismatch. | Critical |
| 3002 | The temperature is too high. | Critical |
| 3003 | Equipment failure. | Critical |
| 3004 | Excessive leakage current in off-grid output. | Critical |
| 3005 | N line grounding fault. | Critical |
